agents.description: Scans the project directory structure using find/ls/bash, catalogs all files by extension and directory, identifies key configuration files (package.json, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, pyproject.toml, etc.), and produces a structured file inventory grouped by technology domain (Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Infrastructure/DevOps, Configuration/Documentation). The inventory includes per-file metadata: path, extension, size, and assigned domain.
agents.output: file_inventory.json — a structured JSON mapping domain → list of file paths with metadata
agents.failure_mode: Single point of failure — if the scanner crashes or produces an incomplete inventory, downstream detectors operate on partial data. Mitigate with a retry loop (max 2 retries) and a fallback inventory generated from simple `find` output.

agents.description: Analyzes Python files (*.py, requirements.txt, setup.py, setup.cfg, pyproject.toml, Pipfile) to detect: programming language version (Python 2/3, 3.11+ features), libraries (via import statements and dependency manifests), frameworks (Django, Flask, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Pydantic), Python-specific design patterns (decorators, context managers, dataclasses), typing conventions (mypy, type hints usage), and testing frameworks (pytest, unittest, tox). Operates only on the Python file subset from the scanner's inventory.
agents.output: python_skills.json — detected Python technologies with per-file occurrence counts
agents.failure_mode: Isolated — crash only affects Python skill detection. Other domain detectors proceed independently.

agents.description: Analyzes JavaScript/TypeScript files (*.js, *.ts, *.jsx, *.tsx, *.mjs, *.cjs, package.json, tsconfig.json) to detect: npm/yarn/pnpm packages, frontend frameworks (React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, Next.js, Nuxt), bundlers (webpack, vite, esbuild, rollup, parcel), testing frameworks (jest, mocha, cypress, playwright), CSS solutions (tailwind, styled-components, CSS modules), and JS/TS-specific patterns (hooks, async/await usage, module system). Operates only on the JS/TS file subset.
agents.output: js_skills.json — detected JavaScript/TypeScript technologies with occurrence counts
agents.failure_mode: Isolated — crash only affects JS/TS skill detection.

agents.description: Analyzes infrastructure and DevOps files (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, k8s/*.yaml, terraform/*.tf, .github/workflows/*.yml, .gitlab-ci.yml, Jenkinsfile, Makefile, CMakeLists.txt) to detect: containerization (Docker, podman), orchestration (Kubernetes, Docker Swarm, Nomad), IaC tools (Terraform, Pulumi, CloudFormation, Ansible), CI/CD platforms (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI, Jenkins), cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure via SDK imports), and monitoring/logging (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK stack). Operates only on the infra/config file subset.
agents.output: infra_skills.json — detected infrastructure technologies with occurrence counts
agents.failure_mode: Isolated — crash only affects infrastructure detection.

agents.description: Analyzes configuration and documentation files (*.yaml, *.yml, *.json, *.toml, *.ini, *.cfg, README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/*.md, LICENSE, CHANGELOG.md) to detect: build systems (Make, CMake, Gradle, Maven, Bazel), documentation frameworks (Sphinx, MkDocs, Docusaurus, Jekyll, Hugo), linting/formatting (eslint, prettier, black, ruff, clang-format), git configuration (.gitignore patterns, .gitattributes, hooks), and project metadata (license type, contribution guidelines, semantic versioning adherence). Operates only on the config/docs file subset.
agents.output: config_docs_skills.json — detected tooling and documentation technologies
agents.failure_mode: Isolated — crash only affects config/docs detection.

agents.description: Merges detection results from all four domain detectors into a unified skill inventory. Resolves conflicting classifications (same skill detected by multiple detectors), deduplicates skill entries by canonical name, computes proficiency levels (detected: 1-2 occurrences, used: 3-10 occurrences, extensively-used: 11+ occurrences) via cross-file frequency analysis across all detector outputs, assigns skill categories (language, framework, library, design_pattern, tooling, domain_knowledge), and validates the merged inventory for completeness and internal consistency. This is the synchronization barrier — it waits for all four detectors to complete before executing.
agents.output: aggregated_skills.json — unified skill inventory with proficiency levels and categories
agents.failure_mode: Single point of failure — aggregator crash loses all detection work. Mitigate by checkpointing each detector's output to disk independently, so aggregator can be re-run from saved detector outputs without re-executing the detection phase.

agents.description: Produces two final output artifacts from the aggregated skill inventory: (1) skills_inventory.json — a structured JSON document with skills grouped by category, each entry containing name, category, proficiency level, occurrence count, source files, and version (if detected); (2) skills_report.md — a human-readable markdown summary report organized by skill category with proficiency distribution statistics, technology stack overview, and notable findings. The markdown report includes a summary table, per-category sections with proficiency badges, and a technology profile narrative.
agents.failure_mode: Isolated — can be re-run from aggregator output. If the report_writer fails, the aggregated_skills.json is preserved on disk.

parallelism_strategy.description: The 4 domain detector agents execute in parallel after project_scanner completes. Each detector receives only its domain's file subset from the inventory, operates independently with zero cross-worker dependencies, and writes its results to a domain-specific JSON file. The aggregator is the synchronization barrier — it waits for all 4 detectors to complete before reading their outputs and merging. This achieves up to 4x wall-clock reduction during the I/O-bound detection phase.
parallelism_strategy.dependency_graph: star_topology — one source (scanner) fans out to N parallel workers, then N workers fan in to one sink (aggregator), then one final sequential step (writer)

pipeline_implementation_guide.overview: The TopologyPipeline scans a software project directory to catalog all technologies in use. It follows a fan-out/fan-in pattern: a single scanner agent catalogs the project structure, then four domain-specialized detector agents analyze different technology domains in parallel, an aggregator merges their findings and computes proficiency levels, and a writer produces the final JSON inventory and markdown report.

nodes.name: project_scanner
nodes.description: Entry point. Runs find/ls to catalog all files, groups them into 4 domains (Python, JS/TS, Infra, Config/Docs), writes file_inventory.json. 15-step max, retry-on-failure with fallback to raw `find` output.

nodes.name: python_detector
nodes.description: Reads Python source files, parses imports and dependency manifests, detects frameworks/libraries/patterns. Writes python_skills.json. 20-step max.

nodes.name: js_detector
nodes.description: Reads JS/TS source files and package.json, detects npm packages, frameworks, bundlers, patterns. Writes js_skills.json. 20-step max.

nodes.name: infra_detector
nodes.description: Reads Dockerfiles, CI/CD configs, IaC manifests, detects containerization, orchestration, cloud, monitoring tools. Writes infra_skills.json. 15-step max.

nodes.name: config_docs_detector
nodes.description: Reads YAML/JSON/TOML configs and markdown docs, detects build systems, doc frameworks, linting/formatting tools, project metadata. Writes config_docs_skills.json. 15-step max.

nodes.name: aggregator
nodes.description: Synchronization barrier. Reads all 4 domain skill files, merges and deduplicates, computes proficiency levels via frequency thresholds, validates consistency. Writes aggregated_skills.json. 20-step max.

nodes.name: report_writer
nodes.description: Terminal node. Reads aggregated_skills.json, produces skills_inventory.json (structured JSON) and skills_report.md (human-readable markdown). 15-step max.

pipeline_implementation_guide.flow_diagram:
┌─────────────────┐
│ project_scanner │  Scan directory, catalog files by domain
│   (15 steps)    │
└────────┬────────┘
         │ file_inventory.json
         │ (fan-out: 4 parallel branches)
    ┌────┼────┬──────────┬──────────────┐
    ▼    ▼    ▼          ▼              ▼
┌──────┐ ┌──────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐
│python│ │  js  │ │  infra   │ │ config_docs      │
│detect│ │detect│ │ detector │ │ _detector        │
│20 stp│ │20 stp│ │ (15 stp) │ │ (15 stp)         │
└──┬───┘ └──┬───┘ └────┬─────┘ └────────┬─────────┘
   │        │          │                │
   │ py     │ js       │ infra          │ config
   │ skills │ skills   │ skills         │ skills
   │ .json  │ .json    │ .json          │ .json
   │        │          │                │
   └────────┼──────────┼────────────────┘
            │  (fan-in barrier) │
            ▼                   ▼
   ┌────────────────────────────────┐
   │         aggregator             │
   │  Merge + dedup + proficiency   │
   │         (20 steps)             │
   └───────────────┬────────────────┘
                   │ aggregated_skills.json
                   ▼
   ┌────────────────────────────────┐
   │         report_writer          │
   │  JSON inventory + MD report    │
   │         (15 steps)             │
   └───────────────┬────────────────┘
                   │
                   ▼
                  END
   Output: skills_inventory.json + skills_report.md
key_design_decisions: Domain-split parallelism: File inventory is split into 4 mutually-exclusive domain groups (Python, JS/TS, Infra, Config/Docs). Each detector only reads files in its domain, reducing per-agent token consumption and avoiding redundant full-project scans.

File-inventory-as-contract: The project_scanner produces a canonical file_inventory.json that serves as the API contract between scanner and detectors. Each detector receives a domain-filtered subset, enabling independent development and testing of each detector against mock inventories.

Checkpointed outputs: Each detector writes its results to a named JSON file on disk before signaling completion. If the aggregator fails, it can be re-run from saved detector outputs without re-executing the entire detection phase — a significant cost saving since detection is the most expensive stage.

Frequency-based proficiency: Proficiency levels (detected/used/extensively-used) are computed by the aggregator using cross-file occurrence counts, not by individual detectors. This centralizes the threshold logic in one place and ensures consistent proficiency assignment across all domains.

No inter-detector communication: Domain detectors operate in complete isolation with no knowledge of each other's findings. This eliminates coordination overhead, prevents cascading failures, and makes the parallelism trivially correct — detectors can run on separate machines if needed.

Scanner retry with fallback: The project_scanner includes a retry loop (max 2 attempts) and a fallback mode that uses raw `find` output if the primary scanning logic fails. This mitigates the single-point-of-failure risk at the pipeline entry point.
pipeline_implementation_guide.configuration_notes: Set CONCURRENCY_LIMIT=4 to match the four domain detectors. For very large projects (>10,000 files), increase max_iterations on python_detector and js_detector to 30. The scanner should use Bash (find, ls, file, wc) for fast directory traversal rather than Read on individual files. Detector agents should batch-read related files (e.g., all Python files in one directory) to minimize tool-call overhead. The aggregator should use `run_command` with `jq` for JSON merging if available, falling back to Read+Write for manual merge. All intermediate JSON files should follow a consistent schema with a `domain` field for traceability.

file_inventory.description: Scanner output — structured file catalog grouped by domain

domain_skills.description: Detector output — skills found within one technology domain

aggregated_skills.description: Aggregator output — merged, deduplicated, proficiency-assigned skills

detector_sources: string (list of domain detector outputs used)

error_handling.scanner_failure: Retry once with a simplified prompt. If still failing, use `find . -type f | head -500` as a raw fallback inventory and log a warning. The pipeline continues with partial file coverage.
error_handling.detector_failure: Failed detectors produce an empty skills array for their domain with an `error` field documenting the failure. The aggregator merges whatever domains completed successfully. A warning is included in the final report for any skipped domains.
error_handling.aggregator_failure: Checkpointed detector outputs on disk mean the aggregator can be re-run independently. The pipeline reports which detector outputs are available and exits with a clear error message listing the missing aggregation step.
error_handling.writer_failure: If aggregated_skills.json exists on disk, the writer can be re-run as a standalone step. The pipeline reports the aggregator output location and exits with instructions for manual report generation.
